1(e)(ii) Line of best fit: Judge by balance of all points on the grid about the candidate√Ę‚ā¨‚ĄĘs line (at least 4 points). Scale markings should be no more than three large squares apart. 1 Plotting of points: All observations in the table must be plotted on the grid. Diameter of plotted points must be √Ę¬©¬Ĺ half a small square (no √Ę‚ā¨Ňďblobs√Ę‚ā¨¬Ě). Points must be plotted to an accuracy of half a small square. 1 Quality: All observations in the table (at least 4) must be plotted on the grid. Trend of points on graph must be correct. It must be possible to draw a straight line that is within √ā¬Ī2.0 cm (to scale) of all the plotted points on the x -axis. 1 9702/33 Cambridge International AS/A Level √Ę‚ā¨‚Äú Mark Scheme PUBLISHED May/June 2019 Page 5 of 8 Question Answer Marks

1(d)(ii) Line of best fit: Judge by balance of all points on the grid about the candidate√Ę‚ā¨‚ĄĘs line (at least 4 points). There must be an even distribution of points either side of the line along the full length. If there are 5 or more points, allow one anomalous point only if clearly indicated by the candidate. Lines must not be kinked or thicker than half a small square. 1

1(d)(iii) Gradient: The hypotenuse of the triangle used must be greater than half the length of the drawn line. The method of calculation must be correct. Do not allow √ĘňÜ‚Ä† x / √ĘňÜ‚Ä† y . Both read-offs must be accurate to half a small square in both the x and y directions. Sign of gradient must match graph. 1 y -intercept: Correct read-off from a point on the line and substituted into y = mx + c. Read-off must be accurate to half a small square in both x and y directions. or Intercept read directly from the graph with read-off at x = 0, accurate to half a small square. 1

1(e) Value of P = candidate√Ę‚ā¨‚ĄĘs gradient and value of Q = candidate√Ę‚ā¨‚ĄĘs intercept. The values must not be fractions. 1 Units for P (s m √Ę‚ā¨‚Äú1 , s cm √Ę‚ā¨‚Äú1 or s mm √Ę‚ā¨‚Äú1 ) and Q (s) correct. 1

question_text: Electromagnetic radiation of a single constant frequency is incident on a metal surface. This causes an electron to be emitted. Explain why the maximum kinetic energy of the electron is independent of the intensity of the incident radiation. [3] (b) Ultraviolet radiation of wavelength 250 nm is incident on the surface of a sheet of zinc. The maximum kinetic energy of the emitted electrons is 1.4 eV.
